name: liblist-compare-perl
title: liblist-compare-perl
summary: Perl module for comparing elements of two or more lists
description:
Perl module for comparing elements of two or more lists
List::Compare lets you compare elements between lists.
This module uses the Carp module which is part of the standard Perl
distribution.  Other than that, there are no module dependencies in
this version of List::Compare.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use liblist-compare-perl because Perl module for comparing elements of two or more lists.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use liblist-compare-perl as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair liblist-compare-perl with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `liblist-compare-perl --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
